§6Cell Workbench§f:
The §6Cell Workbench §f(appliedenergistics2:cell_workbench) is a §6multiblock utility block§f used to §bconfigure ME Storage Cells§f — renaming, locking, partitioning, copying, and managing upgrades. It is essential for any non-trivial AE2 setup.

§6Production Path (network/blocks/cell_workbench.json)§f:
Pattern "aba / cdc / ccc" with the following key ingredients:
- §ba§f: §6Wool §f(any colour) — 2 (top corners)
- §bb§f: §6Calculation Processor §f(appliedenergistics2:material:23) — 1 (top middle)
- §cc§f: §6Iron Ingot §f(ingotIron) — 7 (left, right, and bottom row)
- §cd§f: §6Wooden Chest §f(chestWood) — 1 (centre)
- Yields 1× Cell Workbench

§6Function (TileCellWorkbench.java:45-215)§f:
- §bCell configuration §f: place an §6ME Storage Cell §f in the §6cell slot §f (top of the GUI) and the workbench exposes its §6internal inventory §f
- §bConfig inventory §f: §b63 slots §f for partition items, accessible via the workbench GUI
- §bCopy mode §f: when configured with §6Copy Mode = CLEAR_ON_REMOVE §f (default), removing a cell clears its config; with §6KEEP §f, the config persists
- §bLocked cell §f: the workbench can apply the §6Lock §f upgrade to a cell via shift-right-click
- §bUpgrade management §f: §6Acceleration§f, §6Capacity§f, §6Inverter§f, §6Fuzzy§f, §6Equal Distribution §f upgrades are applied via the workbench
- §bRenaming §f: use the §6Anvil §f interface pattern with the workbench to rename cells

§6Direct Descendants (TaskRetrieval graph)§f:
The jei-graph shows 0 items the Cell Workbench is a component of, but it is referenced in 740 §6use sites §f across the mod pack. Common uses:
- §bPartition setup §f: restrict a cell to a specific list of items
- §bUpgrade swap §f: move upgrades between cells without losing them
- §bLock and rename §f: prevent accidental loss of valuable cells
- §bME Pattern Terminal §f integration: apply patterns to a configured cell

§6Practical Use§f:
- §eTip§f: use the §6Copy Mode §f in conjunction with §6Equal Distribution §f upgrades to create §6buffer cells §f that automatically balance items across the network
- §cWarning§f: the Cell Workbench does §cnot §f provide §bcrafting patterns §f — for that, use a §6Pattern Terminal §f
- §cWarning§f: clearing a §6Partitioned §f cell with §6Copy Mode = CLEAR_ON_REMOVE §f will §close §f the cell's items; always back up before clearing
